title: 4 AI Reporting Tools That Generate Project Reports… | CompanyCam Blog
content: 4 AI Reporting Tools That Generate Project Reports… | CompanyCam Blog No time for a live demo? Watch a pre-recorded demo here. Fill out the info below to get the best demo for your business! Leave this field blank First Name Last Name Phone Number Email Address Company Name Job RoleField Tech/InstallationMarketingOffice AdministrationOperationsOwnerProject ManagementSalesOther Company Size1-34-1011-4950-99100-499500-24992500+ IndustryAdjustingCleaningConcreteElectricalGeneral ConstructionHVACLandscapingPaintingPlumbingPoolProperty ManagementRestoration/RemodelingRoofing/Exteriors/SolarOther Date Submitted Submit Log In Book a Demo Free Trial Start a Free Trial Book a Call Menu RESOURCES / BLOG 4 AI Reporting Tools That Generate Project Reports Without Typing a Word Audrey Ashburner Oct 09, 2025 Max 5 min read Share Tweet Share Email Writing job reports shouldn’t take longer than the job itself. Yet many contractors still spend hours typing daily logs, progress updates, and site summaries after they leave the field. Field AI tools now make it possible to generate job reports automatically using photos and voice notes. Instead of sitting behind a desk, you can walk the site, capture updates, and let AI build the report for you. In this article, we’ll break down four AI tools that help contractors create daily logs, progress reports, and job summaries without typing a word. What Are AI Reporting Tools? AI reporting tools use artificial intelligence to generate structured reports from jobsite data. That data can include photos, voice walkthroughs, project details, checklists, and team updates. Instead of manually compiling information into a report, the software summarizes and formats everything automatically. In construction, AI reporting software helps generate daily logs, progress recaps, and project summaries. It reduces manual entry and eliminates the need to rewrite notes after leaving the job site. For contractors and field service teams, this means faster reporting with fewer mistakes. Four AI Tools That Generate Construction Reports Automatically AI-powered reporting tools are designed to simplify jobsite documentation. Below are four CompanyCam AI tools that help automate daily construction reports and project updates. 1. Walkthrough Note Walkthrough Note allows you to walk the job site, speak your observations, and capture photos in one flow. The tool records your voice, pairs it with images, and automatically organizes everything into a structured report. Instead of jotting down notes and typing later, your report builds itself as you move through the project. For contractors looking for AI tools for generating real-time service reports, this feature keeps updates accurate and current. It reduces time spent rewriting site visit reports and ensures nothing gets missed. 2. AI Summary AI Summary generates a headline-style overview for any Page that includes photos and project details. It adds context to visual documentation without requiring manual typing. This is especially helpful when sharing updates with customers or stakeholders who need quick clarity. For teams searching for AI tools that generate project reports or summarize engineering activity into daily reports, this feature simplifies communication. It removes confusion and presents a clean, readable update every time. 3. Progress Recap Progress Recap compiles selected photos and project updates into a clear overview of what has been completed and what comes next. Instead of digging through folders and notes, you get a snapshot of project status in seconds. This makes weekly meetings and client updates much easier to manage. Contractors comparing AI-generated project reports tools often look for automation plus structure. Progress Recap combines both by organizing photos and summarizing them into a ready-to-share report. 4. Daily Log The automated Daily Log tool creates a comprehensive record of daily activity using photos, summaries, and task lists. It captures workforce activity, material deliveries, and equipment usage without manual typing. The result is a complete daily construction report generated automatically. For anyone searching for the best tool for automating field reports and daily logs, this feature reduces paperwork while improving accountability. Crews can document progress in real time and keep the office informed without extra effort. How AI Reporting Software Works on the Job Site AI reporting software captures jobsite inputs and turns them into detailed documentation. Instead of filling out forms line by line, users record voice notes, upload photos, and select project details. The system processes that information and generates a clean, formatted report. This approach supports automated progress reports and daily construction reporting software needs. Field teams stay focused on the work while the software handles the reporting. It also reduces inconsistencies between crews because reports follow the same structure every time. For contractors asking how to automate daily reports and summaries using AI, the process usually follows three steps: Capture jobsite data through photos and voice.Let AI summarize and format the content.Review and share the finished report. The result is faster reporting without sacrificing clarity. Is Automated Reporting Right for Your Team? AI-generated project reporting tools are especially useful for general contractors, subcontractors, and field service teams. Anyone responsible for documenting daily progress, site visits, or client updates can benefit from automation. Smaller contractors with limited office staff often see the biggest time savings. Construction daily report software powered by AI also helps project managers who oversee multiple sites. Instead of chasing updates from crews, they receive organized, consistent reports automatically. That improves communication and reduces delays tied to missing documentation. FAQ What is the best AI for report generation in construction?The best AI reporting software for construction integrates directly with jobsite workflows. Tools that combine photo documentation, voice capture, and automated summaries are more effective than general AI writing apps because they connect directly to real project data. Can AI generate construction daily reports automatically?Yes, AI-powered daily construction reporting software can generate daily logs automatically from jobsite inputs. Crews capture updates in real time, and the system formats everything into a shareable report without manual typing. How do you automate field reports and daily logs?To automate field reports, use software that allows photo capture, voice walkthroughs, and AI summaries in one platform. This eliminates double entry and reduces the time spent writing reports after hours. Are AI reporting tools secure for construction companies?Most professional AI reporting tools are built with secure cloud storage and permission controls. Always review data policies and access settings before adopting it across your team. The Future of Construction Reporting AI is changing how construction teams handle documentation. Instead of spending hours typing daily logs and progress summaries, crews can generate reports directly from jobsite activity. That shift saves time, reduces errors, and keeps communication consistent across projects. If you are evaluating AI software for your construction business, look for solutions that integrate with your existing workflow. The best AI reporting tools improve documentation without adding complexity. When reporting becomes automatic, your team can focus on building instead of typing. Automate Your Job Reports Generate construction daily reports, progress updates, and field logs in minutes using CompanyCam AI. title: Talk, Snap, Done: How CompanyCam AI Works on the Job | CompanyCam Blog
content: Talk, Snap, Done: How CompanyCam AI Works on the Job | CompanyCam Blog No time for a live demo? Watch a pre-recorded demo here. Fill out the info below to get the best demo for your business! Leave this field blank First Name Last Name Phone Number Email Address Company Name Job RoleField Tech/InstallationMarketingOffice AdministrationOperationsOwnerProject ManagementSalesOther Company Size1-34-1011-4950-99100-499500-24992500+ IndustryAdjustingCleaningConcreteElectricalGeneral ConstructionHVACLandscapingPaintingPlumbingPoolProperty ManagementRestoration/RemodelingRoofing/Exteriors/SolarOther Date Submitted Submit Log In Book a Demo Free Trial Start a Free Trial Book a Call Menu RESOURCES / BLOG Talk, Snap, Done: How CompanyCam AI Works on the Job Audrey Ashburner Sep 10, 2025 Max 3 min read Share Tweet Share Email AI is everywhere right now. Some contractors hear about it and think it’s overhyped. Others worry it’s here to replace people. Neither is true when it’s done right. CompanyCam AI was built for one reason: to take the tedious, time-consuming parts of the job and handle them for you. That’s it. We’re not touching your craftsmanship. We’re not telling you how to run your crew. We’re just giving you more time to do the work only you can do. The Work Stays Yours You still walk the site. You still make the calls. You still decide what’s done and when it’s right. The difference is that you don’t have to spend hours after the job putting all that information into a report or checklist. With CompanyCam AI, you talk to your phone, take photos, and get back to work. The paperwork builds itself. How CompanyCam AI Works CompanyCam AI listens and looks for you. While you capture the job the way you always have, it’s building the documents and updates you need in the background. Here’s what that looks like on the job: Walkthrough Note: Talk through your site inspection while snapping photos. AI combines your voice notes and images into a clean, shareable PDF. Perfect for scopes of work, client recaps, or closeout packages.Captions: Point your camera, say what’s in the shot, and move on. AI adds that caption to the photo instantly. No typos, no later catch-up.AI Checklists (from Voice & Photos): As you walk the site, say what needs to be done. AI creates an instant task list for the crew based on your words and photos.Translations: Speak in your language, and AI translates photo comments and chats for multilingual teams. No more guessing at instructions.Progress Recap: Select the day’s photos and notes. AI builds a timeline-style update for the team or client.Summary: Choose up to 50 photos, and AI writes a job summary that explains the work in context.Daily Log: End the day without sitting down to type. AI outlines what happened and what’s next so everyone knows the plan tomorrow. No Extra Steps, No Learning Curve We know a lot of contractors are skeptical of new tech because it often means changing how you work. This doesn’t. You don’t have to click through menus or learn complicated commands. If you can talk and take photos, you can use CompanyCam AI. Everything happens in the background while you focus on the build. Do the Work That Matters CompanyCam AI isn’t about losing control—it’s about taking it back. You keep doing the work only you can do, and we’ll handle the paperwork nobody wants to do. More time in the field — not behind a deskBetter clarity for crews and clientsLess frustration over missing notes or miscommunicationCleaner, more professional deliverables without hours of formatting The job is still yours. You’re just handing off the parts you never liked doing in the first place. Don’t use CompanyCam yet? Get your team up and running in less than 3 minutes. title: How Contractors Are Turning Walkthroughs Into… | CompanyCam Blog
content: How Contractors Are Turning Walkthroughs Into… | CompanyCam Blog No time for a live demo? Watch a pre-recorded demo here. Fill out the info below to get the best demo for your business! Leave this field blank First Name Last Name Phone Number Email Address Company Name Job RoleField Tech/InstallationMarketingOffice AdministrationOperationsOwnerProject ManagementSalesOther Company Size1-34-1011-4950-99100-499500-24992500+ IndustryAdjustingCleaningConcreteElectricalGeneral ConstructionHVACLandscapingPaintingPlumbingPoolProperty ManagementRestoration/RemodelingRoofing/Exteriors/SolarOther Date Submitted Submit Log In Book a Demo Free Trial Start a Free Trial Book a Call Menu RESOURCES / BLOG How Contractors Are Turning Walkthroughs Into Reports With AI Audrey Ashburner Jan 16, 2026 Max 4 min read Share Tweet Share Email You’ve got 20 jobs running this week. Your foreman is texting you photos with no context. Your PM is asking what happened on the Crawford job on Tuesday. And you’re supposed to have a report ready for a client by noon. The documentation is all there, somewhere. But ​“somewhere” doesn’t close a dispute or keep a client happy. CompanyCam’s AI Walkthrough Note is the job site note taking tool that turns a walk-through into a finished, shareable report. Snap photos, talk through what you see, and the AI organizes everything into a clean page your whole team can access. No typing, no cleanup, no chasing anyone for details. Here’s how it works, and why it matters when you’re running more than a handful of jobs. What is AI Walkthrough Note? Most AI note taking apps were built for people sitting at desks. Walkthrough Note was built for job sites. When you open a project in CompanyCam and start a Walkthrough Note, you walk the job, take photos, and talk about what you see. The AI transcribes your voice, ties your words to the photos you snapped, and builds a formatted page in real time. By the time you’re back at the truck, the report is ready. Three things it handles so you don’t have to: Voice transcription: Talk through what you’re seeing. The AI writes it out, cleans up the ​“ums” and ​“uhs,” and turns it into legible notes.Photo organization: Every photo you take gets attached to the right part of the report. No more hunting through a camera roll to match a picture to a problem.AI-powered editing: The AI formats your notes into a professional summary. What comes out on the other end looks like something a project manager spent an hour on. It took you a walk-through. How AI turns your walkthroughs into reports Start with Pages: In CompanyCam, everything lives under Pages. Once you open your project, head to the Pages tab, hit that + button, and select ​“Walkthrough Note.”Walk and Talk: As you move through the job site, take photos and speak naturally about what you’re seeing. AI Note will organize your notes, automatically creating a detailed AI Summary based on your voice and the pictures you’ve snapped.Instant Organization: All those disjointed thoughts and job site updates you rattle off? AI sorts them into bulleted lists and concise summaries. Your page is built in real-time, making it easy to find and review critical details.Progress at a Glance: No more messy notes or scattered thoughts. When you’re done, the AI note taker gives you a professional report ready to share with your boss, client, or team — complete with all the context, photos, and a clear summary of progress. How to use AI Walkthrough Note This is straightforward enough that a new hire can pick it up on day one. Open a project in the CompanyCam app on your phone. Go to the Pages tab and tap + Page. Select ​“Walkthrough Note” to start documenting in real time. Walk the job, take photos, and talk. That’s it. The AI handles the organization. When you’re done, you’ve got a page with photos, voice-transcribed notes, and an AI-generated summary. Ready to share. Multiple ways to share Once the page is built, sharing it takes seconds. Text a link, send it by email, or download it as a PDF. Whatever format works for the person on the other end. For internal updates, your PM can see everything from the office without calling the field. For client communication, you can send a professional-looking progress report from a job site in the time it takes to walk to your truck. The future of job site reporting The job site report used to mean one thing: someone sat down after a long day and typed out what happened. Notes from memory, photos hunted down from three different phones, a summary that took 45 minutes to produce and still missed half of what actually went on. That version of reporting is going away. The contractors pulling ahead right now are documenting jobs in real time, on-site, while the work is still in front of them. Not because they hired someone to do it. Because the tools finally caught up to the way field work actually happens: moving fast, talking through problems, taking photos as you go. AI doesn’t change the job. It changes what happens to the information after you capture it. The walk-through you were already doing becomes the report. The photos you were already taking become the documentation. The voice notes you were already leaving yourself become the summary your client receives. The businesses that adopt this now are building a compounding advantage. Every job documented cleanly is a dispute avoided, a client kept, a crew held accountable without a single extra conversation. Job site reporting isn’t getting harder. For the contractors paying attention, it’s getting invisible. Stop writing reports. Start walking them. See how CompanyCam turns your job site walkthrough into a shareable report before you reach the truck. title: AI for Jobsite Reporting: Ditch the End-of-Day… | CompanyCam Blog
content: AI for Jobsite Reporting: Ditch the End-of-Day… | CompanyCam Blog No time for a live demo? Watch a pre-recorded demo here. Fill out the info below to get the best demo for your business! Leave this field blank First Name Last Name Phone Number Email Address Company Name Job RoleField Tech/InstallationMarketingOffice AdministrationOperationsOwnerProject ManagementSalesOther Company Size1-34-1011-4950-99100-499500-24992500+ IndustryAdjustingCleaningConcreteElectricalGeneral ConstructionHVACLandscapingPaintingPlumbingPoolProperty ManagementRestoration/RemodelingRoofing/Exteriors/SolarOther Date Submitted Submit Log In Book a Demo Free Trial Start a Free Trial Book a Call Menu RESOURCES / BLOG AI for Jobsite Reporting: Ditch the End-of-Day Paperwork Audrey Ashburner Aug 20, 2025 Max 5 min read Share Tweet Share Email Jobsite reporting has always been one of the toughest parts of end-of-day work. After long shifts, paperwork stacks up and updates don’t always make it back to the office in time. Clients are left waiting for updates, and compliance requirements only add more pressure. AI changes that by handling documentation as the day unfolds. Notes and photos taken in the field are automatically organized into a daily log before the crew even leaves. Reports are ready in real time, so no one is stuck chasing details later. This article shows how AI reporting eases the daily load by reducing paperwork, improving accuracy, and keeping projects moving with less effort — while fitting naturally into the way crews already work. How AI is Changing Jobsite Reporting Jobsite reporting is how crews document the work: what got done, what’s next, safety observations, and client updates. For years, that’s meant handwritten notes, spreadsheets, or long sessions typing everything into a system after the fact. It’s slow, inconsistent, and often the last thing anyone wants to do at the end of a long day. AI changes the process by turning the things crews already capture — photos and spoken notes — into structured reports automatically. There’s no extra typing or formatting, and no need to shuffle details between systems. Crews talk and snap photos, and AI builds the log. The result is reporting that’s faster, more accurate, and consistent across every project. Office staff get hours back, Project Managers see updates as they happen, and leadership finally has a clear, real-time view of the job. 7 Proven AI Strategies for Smarter Jobsite Reporting Reporting is part of the job, but it doesn’t need to slow you down. With AI, the notes and photos you already capture can be turned into complete reports automatically. Here are seven ways it makes that process easier. 1. Capture daily logs as you work On busy jobsites, days run long, and details pile up quickly. When notes or photos are left until the end of the day, information can get scattered. With AI, crews can talk through updates and capture photos as they work, and those voice notes become a daily log automatically. Project Managers see progress the same day, and Office Admins receive consistent reports without extra steps. 2. Turn jobsite photos into progress recaps Photos already document the work. AI takes them further by generating automated progress recap reports that highlight what’s complete and what’s still open. Operations Managers gain a clear view of progress across multiple crews, and client-facing roles can share polished updates in minutes without extra writing. 3. Create professional summaries instantly Closing out a project or phase often involves pulling together photos, notes, and updates into a client-ready package. That work can take hours when handled manually. AI reduces the effort by turning selected photos into a quick AI-generated summary within minutes. Clients see clear proof of work, and internal teams get consistent records that are ready to store or share. 4. Standardize reporting across crews When multiple crews are documenting jobs, reports can end up looking different from site to site. AI evens that out by putting everything into the same clear format, no matter who’s on the job. That way, Operations Managers can review faster, and Owners see reporting they can trust across every project. 5. Close compliance gaps automatically Daily documentation is often required, but it can be difficult to collect and organize consistently. AI builds compliance-ready logs from the notes and photos crews are already capturing. Office Admins don’t have to track down missing details, and Project Managers have complete records ready when inspectors or regulators ask. 6. Give leadership real-time visibility When reports arrive late, problems don’t surface until they’ve already slowed progress. AI changes that by making updates available as the work happens. Project leaders get a clear view of active projects across all sites, and Operation Managers can spot risks early and adjust before small issues turn into costly delays. 7. Free crews to focus on production Crews already juggle a lot on the jobsite, adding paperwork on top takes time away from the work itself. With AI running in the background, logs, recaps, and summaries are created automatically. Crew Leads keep their teams focused on production, and Operations Managers know reporting is done without pulling anyone off the job. Features to Look for in an AI Reporting Tool Not every AI solution is built for the realities of the jobsite. The right tools should cut down on paperwork, keep reports consistent, and make project updates easier to share. When you’re evaluating options, look for tools that can handle the basics of reporting without adding extra steps: Auto reporting: The ability to turn voice notes and photos into daily logs, recaps, and summaries in seconds.Compliance support: Logs and reports formatted to meet documentation requirements automatically.Standardization: Reports that follow the same structure across crews, jobs, and locations.Productivity support: Built-in AI assistants to translate notes, create checklists, or auto-caption photos so nothing gets missed.Visibility & analytics: Dashboards or summaries that give leadership a clear view of progress across multiple sites.Integrations: Seamless connection with the tools you already use for scheduling, project management, or document storage. Reporting That Fits the Way You Already Work AI doesn’t change the work — it changes how it gets reported. Crews capture photos and notes as they always have, but AI turns those inputs into the documentation every role needs: daily logs, progress recaps, and professional summaries. CompanyCam AI builds reports directly from the photos and notes captured on site. That means reporting is complete before anyone leaves the job, and the whole team stays aligned without extra effort. Give AI a Try! Record a quick walkthrough note on CompanyCam and see how fast it becomes a finished daily log. Still Have Questions? Jump into our community forum built for tradespeople. It’s where crews, business owners, and pros swap tips, ask questions, and share real-world advice that actually works. Got something to say or something to figure out? You’ll find your people here. title: Protect Your Business with Daily Logs: What to… | CompanyCam Blog
content: Protect Your Business with Daily Logs: What to… | CompanyCam Blog No time for a live demo? Watch a pre-recorded demo here. Fill out the info below to get the best demo for your business! Leave this field blank First Name Last Name Phone Number Email Address Company Name Job RoleField Tech/InstallationMarketingOffice AdministrationOperationsOwnerProject ManagementSalesOther Company Size1-34-1011-4950-99100-499500-24992500+ IndustryAdjustingCleaningConcreteElectricalGeneral ConstructionHVACLandscapingPaintingPlumbingPoolProperty ManagementRestoration/RemodelingRoofing/Exteriors/SolarOther Date Submitted Submit Log In Book a Demo Free Trial Start a Free Trial Book a Call Menu RESOURCES / BLOG Protect Your Business with Daily Logs: What to Record in Case of a Dispute Audrey Ashburner Oct 09, 2025 Max 3 min read Share Tweet Share Email Daily logs are one of the most important tools you have to protect your business on the job site. When a dispute comes up over delays, damage, or incomplete work, your daily records can make the difference between a costly claim and a clear defense. But how detailed do they really need to be? The short answer: detailed enough to show what happened, who was responsible, and when it happened. Below, we break down what to include in your daily logs and why it matters. Why Your Daily Log Is Your First Line of Protection Daily logs are written records of jobsite activity. If a client claims work wasn’t completed or damage occurred under your watch, your logs can help you prove: Who was on the job that dayWhat work was performedWhat the site conditions wereIf there were delays, and what caused themWhether subcontractors were presentIf safety protocols were followedAny client instructions or changes Without this information, it becomes your word against theirs — and that’s a risk you don’t want to take. What to Include in Your Daily Logs A complete daily log doesn’t need to be long, but it should be consistent and accurate. Use this checklist to make sure you’re covering the key points. 1. Date and Job Name Always include the full date and project name or address. 2. Crew Members On Site List each worker or subcontractor on the job.Note arrival and departure times if possible. 3. Work Performed Summarize the tasks completed that day.Include locations on site and materials used.Mention any milestones reached. 4. Weather and Site Conditions Record temperature, rain, wind, or other conditions that affected the work.Note if any unsafe or unusual site conditions were present. 5. Equipment and Deliveries List major equipment used.Document material deliveries, delays, or shortages. 6. Issues or Delays Note anything that slowed or stopped work, such as:Client changesWaiting on materialsOther trades blocking accessSafety concerns 7. Instructions or Changes Record any client or supervisor instructions.Reference change orders and note if they were verbal or written. 8. Incidents or Hazards Include injuries, near misses, or property damage.Note if an incident report was filed. 9. Photo Documentation Attach or reference photos taken that day.Photos help show work progress, site conditions, and potential risks. The Right Level of Detail for Daily Reports Your log should be factual and clear. Avoid unnecessary opinions or long explanations. Stick to what happened, who was involved, and what actions were taken. Too little detail example: ​“Worked on framing.” This doesn’t show what was completed or how long it took. Better example: ​“Crew framed 2nd floor bedroom and hallway. Installed headers over two windows. Work delayed from 1 – 3 p.m. due to electrician accessing same area.” This level of detail shows progress, coordination issues, and accountability. Tips for Staying Consistent With Daily Logs Use a standardized template or app for logging daily activity.Complete logs at the end of each day — not days later.Train your site supervisor or foreman to write clear, factual entries.Save logs in a shared folder or job management system.Keep copies for at least 3 – 5 years in case of legal claims. Protect Your Business In a dispute, detailed daily logs are one of the best forms of protection you have. They show that your team was organized, professional, and on top of jobsite conditions. Make it a habit to log consistently and accurately. The time you invest each day could save you thousands in the future. USD Contact Sales Everything in Elite + Enterprise-Exclusive Offerings Corporate portal for centralized account management Single sign-on Tailored implementation, onboarding, and training VIP priority support Early access to new offerings Dedicated Enterprise Account Manager Need SSO or a corporate portal? Available on any plan — contact sales ADD-ON +$79/MONTH +$99/MONTH ON ANY PLAN Marketing Suite CompanyCam's Marketing Suite turns your finished jobs into an automatic marketing engine—generating social media posts, boosting your Google ranking, centralizing review management, and building your portfolio and reputation. No extra work required. Book a Demo Compare Plans It all comes down to the features. Pro 79/month Premium 129/month Elite 199/month Documentation Real time photo feed Unlimited projects Unlimited data storage Before & Afters Video capture 5 Minute 10 Minute 10 Minute Photo annotations 4k photo and video LiDAR-powered measurements Accountability Comments Project tasks @Mentions User permissions Checklists Collaborators Digital Signatures Organization Integrations Photo tags Project labels Marketing And Sales Galleries PDF photo reports Showcases Payments Logo stickers Website Gallery Website Map Dual Video Mode Customer Reviews Advanced Features AI Features 10 Credits Report Templates Project Templates Checklist Templates Page Templates 10 minute video Company Dashboard Services Dedicated success rep Chat and email support Frequently Asked Questions What if I only need one user seat? CompanyCam is a great tool for any contractor, but to get the most out of our communication and collaboration tools you need someone to communicate and collaborate with. That being said, we won’t force you — you’ll have two empty seats that you can leave empty forever, OR fill at any time with the people you work with the most, whether that is someone in your office or a subcontractor. What’s the difference between monthly and annual subscriptions? The SAVINGS you get on the annual plan 🤑. Plus, with the annual plan you pay up front for an entire year, and you don’t get billed again until your year is up. On the monthly plan, you pay month-to-month for more flexibility. Adding and removing users works differently as well. For an annual subscription, your first bill is based on how many users you have. Then if and when you add a user, you’ll be charged a one-time amount, prorated starting the day you add them. When you deactivate a user on an annual plan, you’ll have an empty seat that you can fill with a new user. If you never fill that empty seat, your next annual bill will reflect your current user number. Which plan should I choose if I want to use the AI features? Our Pro subscription gives you 10 credits to try out our AI-powered actions, shared across your entire company. When you use an AI action, your number of credits will go down, and deleting an action you’ve already used won’t increase the number of credits you have. For unlimited AI access, choose a Premium or Elite plan! How long do you store my photos? Forever! As long as you’re a CompanyCam customer, we won’t delete any of your photos. If you stay with us for 10 years, you’ll have 10 years worth of photos, nicely organized right where you can find them. If you ever quit using CompanyCam, you can download your photos before you cancel. If I dismiss or lose an employee do I lose their photos? Nope. Every photo that anyone at your company takes using the CompanyCam app is stored in the cloud in your account. If you dismiss an employee, just delete them from your account. You will still have all of their photos, but they won’t be able to access them. Does CompanyCam use a lot of memory on my phone? Not at all. CompanyCam saves your work photos in the cloud so they don’t clutter up your camera roll or take up space on your device. Are my photos secure? Very secure. We use Amazon’s S3 Cloud to store photos securely online. We also use SSL encryption to form a secure connection with the server while we’re transferring photos. Only you (and your company) have access to your photos. Can I switch from an annual to monthly subscription? You sure can. At the end of your one-year subscription, you can switch back to monthly before you auto-renew and get charged for another year. You can cancel your annual subscription at any point, but you’ll still have access to your account until the year is over. Do annual subscriptions auto-renew? Yes — we will send you a reminder email before you are charged. Is the CompanyCam app available on iOS and Android devices? Yep! Two features have capabilities that require specific devices. The ability to record in Dual Video Mode is exclusive to iOS users. The videos generated can then be viewed by anyone!Similarly, capturing photos in LiDAR Mode requires an iOS device with a LiDAR sensor, but once a photo is taken in a qualifying device with LiDAR Mode enabled, anyone on the team can use those photos to get measurements. Will LiDAR measuring work on my device? Great question!Your team will need at least one device with a LiDAR sensor to capture photos in LiDAR Mode. Once a photo is taken in a qualifying device with LiDAR Mode enabled, anyone on the team can use those photos to get measurements — from any device with CompanyCam on it.Here’s a list of devices with a LiDAR sensor:iPhone 12 Pro and lateriPhone 12 Pro Max and lateriPad Pro 13-inch (M4) and 11-inch (M4)iPad Pro 12.9‑inch (4th generation) and lateriPad Pro 11-inch (2nd generation) and later

Available on any plan — contact sales ADD-ON +$79/MONTH +$99/MONTH ON ANY PLAN Marketing Suite CompanyCam's Marketing Suite turns your finished jobs into an automatic marketing engine—generating social media posts, boosting your Google ranking, centralizing review management, and building your portfolio and reputation. No extra work required. Book a Demo Compare Plans It all comes down to the features. Pro 79/month Premium 129/month Elite 199/month Documentation Real time photo feed Unlimited projects Unlimited data storage Before & Afters Video capture 5 Minute 10 Minute 10 Minute Photo annotations 4k photo and video LiDAR-powered measurements Accountability Comments Project tasks @Mentions User permissions Checklists Collaborators Digital Signatures Organization Integrations Photo tags Project labels Marketing And Sales Galleries PDF photo reports Showcases Payments Logo stickers Website Gallery Website Map Dual Video Mode Customer Reviews Advanced Features AI Features 10 Credits Report Templates Project Templates Checklist Templates Page Templates 10 minute video Company Dashboard Services Dedicated success rep Chat and email support Frequently Asked Questions What if I only need one user seat? CompanyCam is a great tool for any contractor, but to get the most out of our communication and collaboration tools you need someone to communicate and collaborate with. That being said, we won’t force you — you’ll have two empty seats that you can leave empty forever, OR fill at any time with the people you work with the most, whether that is someone in your office or a subcontractor. What’s the difference between monthly and annual subscriptions? The SAVINGS you get on the annual plan 🤑. Plus, with the annual plan you pay up front for an entire year, and you don’t get billed again until your year is up. On the monthly plan, you pay month-to-month for more flexibility. Adding and removing users works differently as well. For an annual subscription, your first bill is based on how many users you have. Then if and when you add a user, you’ll be charged a one-time amount, prorated starting the day you add them. When you deactivate a user on an annual plan, you’ll have an empty seat that you can fill with a new user. If you never fill that empty seat, your next annual bill will reflect your current user number. Which plan should I choose if I want to use the AI features? Our Pro subscription gives you 10 credits to try out our AI-powered actions, shared across your entire company. When you use an AI action, your number of credits will go down, and deleting an action you’ve already used won’t increase the number of credits you have. For unlimited AI access, choose a Premium or Elite plan! How long do you store my photos? Forever! As long as you’re a CompanyCam customer, we won’t delete any of your photos. If you stay with us for 10 years, you’ll have 10 years worth of photos, nicely organized right where you can find them. If you ever quit using CompanyCam, you can download your photos before you cancel. If I dismiss or lose an employee do I lose their photos? Nope. Every photo that anyone at your company takes using the CompanyCam app is stored in the cloud in your account. If you dismiss an employee, just delete them from your account. You will still have all of their photos, but they won’t be able to access them. Does CompanyCam use a lot of memory on my phone? Not at all. CompanyCam saves your work photos in the cloud so they don’t clutter up your camera roll or take up space on your device. Are my photos secure? Very secure. We use Amazon’s S3 Cloud to store photos securely online. We also use SSL encryption to form a secure connection with the server while we’re transferring photos. Only you (and your company) have access to your photos. Can I switch from an annual to monthly subscription? You sure can. At the end of your one-year subscription, you can switch back to monthly before you auto-renew and get charged for another year. You can cancel your annual subscription at any point, but you’ll still have access to your account until the year is over. Do annual subscriptions auto-renew? Yes — we will send you a reminder email before you are charged. Is the CompanyCam app available on iOS and Android devices? Yep! Two features have capabilities that require specific devices. The ability to record in Dual Video Mode is exclusive to iOS users. The videos generated can then be viewed by anyone!Similarly, capturing photos in LiDAR Mode requires an iOS device with a LiDAR sensor, but once a photo is taken in a qualifying device with LiDAR Mode enabled, anyone on the team can use those photos to get measurements. Will LiDAR measuring work on my device? Great question!Your team will need at least one device with a LiDAR sensor to capture photos in LiDAR Mode. Once a photo is taken in a qualifying device with LiDAR Mode enabled, anyone on the team can use those photos to get measurements — from any device with CompanyCam on it.Here’s a list of devices with a LiDAR sensor:iPhone 12 Pro and lateriPhone 12 Pro Max and lateriPad Pro 13-inch (M4) and 11-inch (M4)iPad Pro 12.9‑inch (4th generation) and lateriPad Pro 11-inch (2nd generation) and later

You can cancel your annual subscription at any point, but you’ll still have access to your account until the year is over. Do annual subscriptions auto-renew? Yes — we will send you a reminder email before you are charged. Is the CompanyCam app available on iOS and Android devices? Yep! Two features have capabilities that require specific devices. The ability to record in Dual Video Mode is exclusive to iOS users. The videos generated can then be viewed by anyone!Similarly, capturing photos in LiDAR Mode requires an iOS device with a LiDAR sensor, but once a photo is taken in a qualifying device with LiDAR Mode enabled, anyone on the team can use those photos to get measurements. Will LiDAR measuring work on my device? Great question!Your team will need at least one device with a LiDAR sensor to capture photos in LiDAR Mode. Once a photo is taken in a qualifying device with LiDAR Mode enabled, anyone on the team can use those photos to get measurements — from any device with CompanyCam on it.Here’s a list of devices with a LiDAR sensor:iPhone 12 Pro and lateriPhone 12 Pro Max and lateriPad Pro 13-inch (M4) and 11-inch (M4)iPad Pro 12.9‑inch (4th generation) and lateriPad Pro 11-inch (2nd generation) and later

CompanyCam is a great tool for any contractor, but to get the most out of our communication and collaboration tools you need someone to communicate and collaborate with. That being said, we won’t force you — you’ll have two empty seats that you can leave empty forever, OR fill at any time with the people you work with the most, whether that is someone in your office or a subcontractor. What’s the difference between monthly and annual subscriptions? The SAVINGS you get on the annual plan 🤑. Plus, with the annual plan you pay up front for an entire year, and you don’t get billed again until your year is up. On the monthly plan, you pay month-to-month for more flexibility. Adding and removing users works differently as well. For an annual subscription, your first bill is based on how many users you have. Then if and when you add a user, you’ll be charged a one-time amount, prorated starting the day you add them. When you deactivate a user on an annual plan, you’ll have an empty seat that you can fill with a new user. If you never fill that empty seat, your next annual bill will reflect your current user number. Which plan should I choose if I want to use the AI features? Our Pro subscription gives you 10 credits to try out our AI-powered actions, shared across your entire company. When you use an AI action, your number of credits will go down, and deleting an action you’ve already used won’t increase the number of credits you have. For unlimited AI access, choose a Premium or Elite plan! How long do you store my photos? Forever! As long as you’re a CompanyCam customer, we won’t delete any of your photos. If you stay with us for 10 years, you’ll have 10 years worth of photos, nicely organized right where you can find them. If you ever quit using CompanyCam, you can download your photos before you cancel. If I dismiss or lose an employee do I lose their photos? Nope. Every photo that anyone at your company takes using the CompanyCam app is stored in the cloud in your account. If you dismiss an employee, just delete them from your account. You will still have all of their photos, but they won’t be able to access them. Does CompanyCam use a lot of memory on my phone? Not at all. CompanyCam saves your work photos in the cloud so they don’t clutter up your camera roll or take up space on your device. Are my photos secure? Very secure. We use Amazon’s S3 Cloud to store photos securely online. We also use SSL encryption to form a secure connection with the server while we’re transferring photos. Only you (and your company) have access to your photos. Can I switch from an annual to monthly subscription? You sure can. At the end of your one-year subscription, you can switch back to monthly before you auto-renew and get charged for another year. You can cancel your annual subscription at any point, but you’ll still have access to your account until the year is over. Do annual subscriptions auto-renew? Yes — we will send you a reminder email before you are charged. Is the CompanyCam app available on iOS and Android devices? Yep! Two features have capabilities that require specific devices. The ability to record in Dual Video Mode is exclusive to iOS users. The videos generated can then be viewed by anyone!Similarly, capturing photos in LiDAR Mode requires an iOS device with a LiDAR sensor, but once a photo is taken in a qualifying device with LiDAR Mode enabled, anyone on the team can use those photos to get measurements. Will LiDAR measuring work on my device? Great question!Your team will need at least one device with a LiDAR sensor to capture photos in LiDAR Mode. Once a photo is taken in a qualifying device with LiDAR Mode enabled, anyone on the team can use those photos to get measurements — from any device with CompanyCam on it.Here’s a list of devices with a LiDAR sensor:iPhone 12 Pro and lateriPhone 12 Pro Max and lateriPad Pro 13-inch (M4) and 11-inch (M4)iPad Pro 12.9‑inch (4th generation) and lateriPad Pro 11-inch (2nd generation) and later

Available on any plan — contact sales ADD-ON +$79/MONTH +$99/MONTH ON ANY PLAN Marketing Suite CompanyCam's Marketing Suite turns your finished jobs into an automatic marketing engine—generating social media posts, boosting your Google ranking, centralizing review management, and building your portfolio and reputation. No extra work required. Book a Demo Compare Plans It all comes down to the features. Pro 79/month Premium 129/month Elite 199/month Documentation Real time photo feed Unlimited projects Unlimited data storage Before & Afters Video capture 5 Minute 10 Minute 10 Minute Photo annotations 4k photo and video LiDAR-powered measurements Accountability Comments Project tasks @Mentions User permissions Checklists Collaborators Digital Signatures Organization Integrations Photo tags Project labels Marketing And Sales Galleries PDF photo reports Showcases Payments Logo stickers Website Gallery Website Map Dual Video Mode Customer Reviews Advanced Features AI Features 10 Credits Report Templates Project Templates Checklist Templates Page Templates 10 minute video Company Dashboard Services Dedicated success rep Chat and email support Frequently Asked Questions What if I only need one user seat? CompanyCam is a great tool for any contractor, but to get the most out of our communication and collaboration tools you need someone to communicate and collaborate with. That being said, we won’t force you — you’ll have two empty seats that you can leave empty forever, OR fill at any time with the people you work with the most, whether that is someone in your office or a subcontractor. What’s the difference between monthly and annual subscriptions? The SAVINGS you get on the annual plan 🤑. Plus, with the annual plan you pay up front for an entire year, and you don’t get billed again until your year is up. On the monthly plan, you pay month-to-month for more flexibility. Adding and removing users works differently as well. For an annual subscription, your first bill is based on how many users you have. Then if and when you add a user, you’ll be charged a one-time amount, prorated starting the day you add them. When you deactivate a user on an annual plan, you’ll have an empty seat that you can fill with a new user. If you never fill that empty seat, your next annual bill will reflect your current user number. Which plan should I choose if I want to use the AI features? Our Pro subscription gives you 10 credits to try out our AI-powered actions, shared across your entire company. When you use an AI action, your number of credits will go down, and deleting an action you’ve already used won’t increase the number of credits you have. For unlimited AI access, choose a Premium or Elite plan! How long do you store my photos? Forever! As long as you’re a CompanyCam customer, we won’t delete any of your photos. If you stay with us for 10 years, you’ll have 10 years worth of photos, nicely organized right where you can find them. If you ever quit using CompanyCam, you can download your photos before you cancel. If I dismiss or lose an employee do I lose their photos? Nope. Every photo that anyone at your company takes using the CompanyCam app is stored in the cloud in your account. If you dismiss an employee, just delete them from your account. You will still have all of their photos, but they won’t be able to access them. Does CompanyCam use a lot of memory on my phone? Not at all. CompanyCam saves your work photos in the cloud so they don’t clutter up your camera roll or take up space on your device. Are my photos secure? Very secure. We use Amazon’s S3 Cloud to store photos securely online. We also use SSL encryption to form a secure connection with the server while we’re transferring photos. Only you (and your company) have access to your photos. Can I switch from an annual to monthly subscription? You sure can. At the end of your one-year subscription, you can switch back to monthly before you auto-renew and get charged for another year. You can cancel your annual subscription at any point, but you’ll still have access to your account until the year is over. Do annual subscriptions auto-renew? Yes — we will send you a reminder email before you are charged. Is the CompanyCam app available on iOS and Android devices? Yep! Two features have capabilities that require specific devices. The ability to record in Dual Video Mode is exclusive to iOS users. The videos generated can then be viewed by anyone!Similarly, capturing photos in LiDAR Mode requires an iOS device with a LiDAR sensor, but once a photo is taken in a qualifying device with LiDAR Mode enabled, anyone on the team can use those photos to get measurements. Will LiDAR measuring work on my device? Great question!Your team will need at least one device with a LiDAR sensor to capture photos in LiDAR Mode. Once a photo is taken in a qualifying device with LiDAR Mode enabled, anyone on the team can use those photos to get measurements — from any device with CompanyCam on it.Here’s a list of devices with a LiDAR sensor:iPhone 12 Pro and lateriPhone 12 Pro Max and lateriPad Pro 13-inch (M4) and 11-inch (M4)iPad Pro 12.9‑inch (4th generation) and lateriPad Pro 11-inch (2nd generation) and later

title: Case Study: LaFata & Son Excavating | CompanyCam
content: Case Study: LaFata & Son Excavating | CompanyCam No time for a live demo? Watch a pre-recorded demo here. Fill out the info below to get the best demo for your business! Leave this field blank First Name Last Name Phone Number Email Address Company Name Job RoleField Tech/InstallationMarketingOffice AdministrationOperationsOwnerProject ManagementSalesOther Company Size1-34-1011-4950-99100-499500-24992500+ IndustryAdjustingCleaningConcreteElectricalGeneral ConstructionHVACLandscapingPaintingPlumbingPoolProperty ManagementRestoration/RemodelingRoofing/Exteriors/SolarOther Date Submitted Submit Log In Book a Demo Free Trial Start a Free Trial Book a Call Menu RESOURCES / CASE STUDIES / LAFATA AND SON EXCAVATING “It is a bridge between the office and the field. It ties them together in a way that keeps us organized and accountable and it keeps all our things in order.” Building operational control across multiple crews and job sites. Industry General Location Guildford, Connecticut Employees 10+ Customer Since 2022 Checklist Created 2,379 Tag Created 1,766 The Problem Staying in control when everything gets buried LaFata & Son Inc. has been moving dirt along Connecticut’s shoreline for more than 50 years. Anthony LaFata took over the company’s excavation division in the 1980s and has grown it into a full-service site work operation serving residential developers, municipalities, and commercial contractors across the region. Over time, Anthony expanded the business into a second company—A&D Equipment—selling the same excavator attachments the LaFata crews rely on every day. The work is technical, time-sensitive, and almost entirely invisible once it’s done. Utility lines, septic systems, foundation stub-outs — everything gets backfilled and buried. If a crew couldn’t locate something later, or a customer disputed whether work was done at all, the only option was to dig it up and find out. Without a reliable documentation system, that’s exactly what happened. “Before CompanyCam, we took photos, but conveying that information was super difficult and super problematic,” Anthony said. Without a central place to store and share them, photos stayed trapped on personal phones. There was no standard for what to document, no easy way to share photos with the office, and no way to find them later or tie them to a specific job. The company also had no way to protect itself when disputes arose. If a customer claimed work wasn’t done, or a contractor blamed LaFata for damage that happened overnight, there was no proof either way. “Before CompanyCam, we would’ve been on the hook because we would’ve had no way to prove it wasn’t us.” Donna LaFata — President, A&D Equipment, LaFata & Son Excavating The Solution Documentation that works as hard as the crew does Anthony tried other technology platforms over the years, spending significant money on servers, software, and training that never stuck. CompanyCam caught his attention through social media, and after a trial, the whole team was using it within weeks. For a company with multiple crews spread across several active job sites at any given time, having one place where every photo, update, and job record lived wasn’t just convenient, it was the only way to stay in control. The crew now follows a consistent routine: photos at the start of every day, throughout the job every 15 – 20 minutes, and again before leaving the site. Every delivery receipt, daily job sheet, and material purchase gets photographed and tied to the project. Operations manager Luke Cormier can monitor any job from his phone without driving there. “It’s nice to be on a job, but also be able to monitor what’s going on at another job. I can just go on my phone, look at the pictures and see this is going on.” The documentation routine has paid off directly. When something goes wrong on a job site or disputes arise, LaFata’s crew has the answers. Their photo record proves their work and protects their reputation. When a gas meter was struck overnight on one of LaFata’s active job sites, the crew’s end-of-day photos proved their equipment hadn’t moved. LaFata escaped a $10,000 repair bill because they had the photos to prove the damage wasn’t their fault. “It’s not just a photo, it’s a document…Think of it as a story that’s being told live.” Anthony LaFata — President, LaFata & Son Inc., LaFata & Son Excavating Insights Control at scale, with real savings The business impact isn’t just about avoiding bad outcomes. CompanyCam changed how LaFata & Son manages work across crews and jobs every day. Donna tracks field progress from her desk each morning without a single phone call to the field. Anthony notes that having visibility throughout the day lets him catch problems before they escalate, which wasn’t possible before. “I’d have to run to the job and that isn’t two minutes away. It’s sometimes 20 minutes, a half hour away. We’re going to lose how much time trying to come up with a solution.” Having photo proof of every job means Luke can verify what was done, where, and to what depth before anyone picks up a shovel preventing rework an estimated two to three times per week. The as-built process alone — once a three-person job involving tape measures and hand-drawn diagrams — is now one person with a phone. When a septic system installed two years earlier needed service, Luke found the D‑box in minutes instead of spending half a day digging. Anthony estimates CompanyCam saves LaFata & Son between $50,000 and $100,000 per year across liability protection, reduced rework, and operational efficiency. For a company where nearly every job disappears underground, that proof is the product. 84,265 Photos 681 Projects “Freedom. It frees up my time and just makes us run more efficiently, effectively, and easily.” Anthony LaFata — President, LaFata & Son Inc., LaFata & Son Excavating See CompanyCam in Action Schedule a live walkthrough to see how it all works. title: Case Study: Kyro Builders | CompanyCam
content: Case Study: Kyro Builders | CompanyCam No time for a live demo? Watch a pre-recorded demo here. Fill out the info below to get the best demo for your business! Leave this field blank First Name Last Name Phone Number Email Address Company Name Job RoleField Tech/InstallationMarketingOffice AdministrationOperationsOwnerProject ManagementSalesOther Company Size1-34-1011-4950-99100-499500-24992500+ IndustryAdjustingCleaningConcreteElectricalGeneral ConstructionHVACLandscapingPaintingPlumbingPoolProperty ManagementRestoration/RemodelingRoofing/Exteriors/SolarOther Date Submitted Submit Log In Book a Demo Free Trial Start a Free Trial Book a Call Menu RESOURCES / CASE STUDIES / KYRO BUILDERS “We’re saving a couple hundred thousand dollars a year by covering our tails, by having documented evidence.” Scaling a business through trust, quality, and partnership. Industry Roofing Location Chesapeake, VA Employees 60 Customer Since 2022 Hours Saved Per Week 40 Labels Used 7,500 The Problem Maintaining quality while away from the job site Kyro Builders is a Virginia-based roofing, solar, and exterior company, co-founded by firefighters Kyle Billips and Robert Oliver in 2014. Each was taking on remodeling jobs during off days from the station to make some extra money, helping one another on each other’s jobs. But as word of the quality and reliability of the work spread from family to friends to friends of friends, they decided to form Kyro Builders and hired their fellow firefighters as some of their first employees. With their team’s unique background in emergency services, they knew firsthand the importance of documenting everything. The team was capturing photos on their personal phones, but they kept running into issues with limited storage, broken phones, and employees not wanting to clutter their phones with work photos. They even tried handing out work phones, but more often than not, those didn’t make it out of the truck. Because they lacked photo proof, they estimated that they were paying ​“a couple hundred thousand” dollars for preexisting damage—ripped hot tub covers, cracked driveways, and torn window screens — that homeowners may or may not have known were damaged before their team’s arrival. And since they are full-time firefighters, they needed a system in place to confidently rely on the team to manage jobs while they have limited access to their phones during their 24-hour shifts. “One of our sayings is, ​‘If you don’t document it, it never happened.’” Robert Oliver — co-founder, Kyro Builders The Solution Easy to implement field tools Kyro Builders first heard about CompanyCam through their partnership with Atlas Roofing. They credit the trusting relationship they’ve built with the Atlas team as part of the reason for giving CompanyCam a go: “[Our Atlas rep] said, ​‘Hey, we’re partnering with CompanyCam. They’re great. If you’re not using it, you really need to check it out. It’s the best way to manage photos.” After rolling it out, ​“the guys immediately loved it,” and management started getting more and more photos from the job site. As part of their process, their team walks the house twice, documenting every screen, shutter, and gutter before they even put on their tool belts. These photos, along with time and date stamps, give them the confidence that everything is being documented, so that everyone can operate on the same page. Their documentation even helped them get a $40,000 lawsuit dismissed. Consistent documentation also helps with conversations around change orders. As roofing jobs progress, their team numbers damaged materials with spray paint, snaps a photo in CompanyCam, and explains why it’s being replaced. They then install the new decking, take another picture, and continue along. They then share these with customers to explain why they had additional line items on their invoice: ​“This conversation is a lot easier once people can see why. [Customers] appreciate that.” “It’s really solved a lot of the issues. And as a business owner, I no longer had to keep hearing the excuses as to why they weren’t doing what they’re supposed to be doing.” Robert Oliver — co-founder, Kyro Builders Insights Measurable time and money savings The team has grown to over 60 employees and is completing 12 – 20 jobs per week. They’ve added departments that encompass a range of projects, including commercial and HOA projects, residential maintenance, and insurance work. As they grow into their second decade in operation, the owners can focus on diversifying their business and staying ahead of the curve, rather than worrying about the fundamentals of having proper documentation or avoiding communication breakdowns due to missing photos. Between their CRM Acculynx and CompanyCam, they estimate that they save about three administrative roles because of how efficiently their team can run the business with these systems, giving the owners peace of mind that things are being taken care of while they’re on duty. They are expanding their fleet, investing in equipment to produce some of their own building materials, and doubling down on training for installation and sales crews so that they can continue to be the leader in their market in providing a great product and experience that both their customers and employees can be proud of. 35+ Users 510,000 Photos 9,500 Projects “What we’re really trying to maintain is being an honest contractor. And part of being an honest contractor, to me, is having that documentation and not being afraid to take those pictures and share that information.” Robert Oliver — co-founder, Kyro Builders See CompanyCam in Action Schedule a live walkthrough to see how it all works. title: Case Study: Tri-Square Construction | CompanyCam
content: Case Study: Tri-Square Construction | CompanyCam No time for a live demo? Watch a pre-recorded demo here. Fill out the info below to get the best demo for your business! Leave this field blank First Name Last Name Phone Number Email Address Company Name Job RoleField Tech/InstallationMarketingOffice AdministrationOperationsOwnerProject ManagementSalesOther Company Size1-34-1011-4950-99100-499500-24992500+ IndustryAdjustingCleaningConcreteElectricalGeneral ConstructionHVACLandscapingPaintingPlumbingPoolProperty ManagementRestoration/RemodelingRoofing/Exteriors/SolarOther Date Submitted Submit Log In Book a Demo Free Trial Start a Free Trial Book a Call Menu RESOURCES / CASE STUDIES / TRI SQUARE CONSTRUCTION “Field-to-office relations have just been next level.” Better field-to-office communication reduces rework, speeds invoicing. Industry General Location California & Nevada Employees 60 Customer Since 2022 Checklists Completed 3,300 Photo Comments 3,000+ The Problem Tri-Square Construction is a construction company specializing in design, cell tower construction, and emergency maintenance services of wireless communication sites. Founded in 2002, they’ve grown into a leader in the California and Nevada markets, with 60 employees managing more than two dozen daily projects. Because of the multiple stakeholders involved in every job, this industry has always relied on photos to ensure quality control and accountability. Tri-Square’s documentation process revolved around crews capturing photos with digital cameras, which would get dropped off at the office at the end of the day, where the team would then manually upload and organize them on an internal server, and later, with native phone cameras and text/​email. This process worked well enough, but it didn’t allow for quick field-to-office communication, so any missing photo documentation resulted in shuffling schedules, redeploying crews, eating extra equipment rentals, and, most importantly, not getting paid on time. And as mobile tech advanced, carriers and owners required quicker turnarounds for proof of work — sometimes within 24 hours of crews leaving a job site. They began having techs upload photos to a Google Drive, but the process was clunky, and key photos were still missing. “Our industry is basically 100 percent defined by photographs […] We do not get paid without turning in photos.” Andy Perez — Closeout Manager, Tri-Square Construction The Solution Increased field-to-office collaboration The team was aware of CompanyCam. However, many carriers have their own required apps for submitting photos, so they didn’t want to add another. But after an especially bad project closeout with a lot of missing documentation, the Tri-Square team adopted CompanyCam as part of their process. With photos instantly flowing from the field to the office, they can more quickly answer questions from technicians. More importantly, they can identify what photo documentation is missing that could cause a closeout package to fail. All new hires are trained on how to use CompanyCam before they ever go up a tower, so that when they’re up there, they can work efficiently in and outside of the app. All employees can access past jobs to review and see how different carriers require work to be done. It’s become sort of a ​“social media feed” for the team: “The guys can look back at past sites and say, ​‘I’ve only worked on an AT&T [tower] and now I’m at a Verizon.’ They can go through those [and] see other work, see how other people did it, and they’re tagging each other [to learn more about the job].” “It’s like a new ecosystem inside our company.” Andy Perez — Closeout Manager, Tri-Square Construction Insights Reduced rework and rejections Since implementation, Tri-Square has seen a ​“significant” increase in the number of closeout packages that are accepted on the first submission. Which is one of the ​“key metrics,” because it allows them to bill for their work and get paid sooner. Because they can see and communicate in real time, they’ve cut down on the number of redeployments, and the redeployments that do happen can often be completed from the ground — saving them on equipment rentals and extra labor costs. The Tri-Square team credits CompanyCam with helping their company culture become one that sets a clear standard for how the work should be done, embraces self-directed education, and encourages collaboration between those out on the job. 50 Users 220,000 Photos 1,000+ Projects “Field-to-office relations have just been next level. It just goes to everybody — they can tag us on the photo, and we can go directly there and solve problems.” Andy Perez — Closeout Manager, Tri-Square Construction See CompanyCam in Action Schedule a live walkthrough to see how it all works. title: Case Study: Footprints Floors | CompanyCam
content: Case Study: Footprints Floors | CompanyCam No time for a live demo? Watch a pre-recorded demo here. Fill out the info below to get the best demo for your business! Leave this field blank First Name Last Name Phone Number Email Address Company Name Job RoleField Tech/InstallationMarketingOffice AdministrationOperationsOwnerProject ManagementSalesOther Company Size1-34-1011-4950-99100-499500-24992500+ IndustryAdjustingCleaningConcreteElectricalGeneral ConstructionHVACLandscapingPaintingPlumbingPoolProperty ManagementRestoration/RemodelingRoofing/Exteriors/SolarOther Date Submitted Submit Log In Book a Demo Free Trial Start a Free Trial Book a Call Menu RESOURCES / CASE STUDIES / FOOTPRINTS FLOORS “It’s a win-win. It’s a no-brainer.” Powering franchise growth through photo documentation. Industry Flooring Location Denver Customer Since 2022 Favorite Feature Before & After The Problem Too many photos, not enough organization Founded in 2008 by Bryan Park, Footprints Floors has grown from a Denver-area flooring crew to the largest installation company in the United States, completing more than 60,000 projects in 40 states since day one. One key to their growth is their fully comprehensive model. Franchisees aren’t just getting the logo and a market; they have a full-time corporate staff behind them taking care of many of the backend things so that they can focus on managing jobs, building customer relationships, training their teams, and putting in the best floors possible. However, with quick growth came a need to scale communication and collaboration across the dozens of daily jobs in any market. Photos and job site documentation were always important to the Footprints Floors team, but without an easy way to keep them organized and accessible, photos and notes would fill up team members’ camera rolls, but they wouldn’t always get shared with the marketing, sales, subcontractors, or clients. “When you start storing up hundreds, if not thousands of pictures, on your phone… all the pictures start to kind of look the same.” Bryan Park — Founder & CEO, Footprints Floors The Solution Centralized, organized, and accessible job site documentation Rolling out new software in a franchise can be difficult. Up front, new tech might look like an additional expense or seem that corporate is getting too involved in a franchisee’s day-to-day operations. So Bryan and his team strive only to recommend tools that answer two questions: Does it improve the customer’s experience? Does it make franchisees’ life easier? CompanyCam met both and more. By centralizing all photos and videos in CompanyCam, the process from first contact to final nail is streamlined for crew members and customers. Subcontractors can see photos of the job before they arrive, so they know what to expect and what tools to bring. Customers also appreciate the detail that goes into documenting their space, giving them peace of mind in their decision to work with Footprints.Marketing Franchisees can easily add updated project photos to their website via Showcases and share before and after shots on Facebook and Instagram. Sales Samples can only show so much. With CompanyCam, sales teams can quickly pull up real-world examples of what that floor looks like in a home. These photos move customers out of a logical headspace (This could be expensive, do I really need this?) to a more excited, emotional one (Wow, my home could actually look like this!). Production Footprints installation crews capture photos of preexisting damage, material prep, and installation progress that they share with customers for warranty information. These photos help crews work more efficiently and protect them from liability. “We’re not walking in blind or being surprised by something… [Crews have] seen the pictures, all the pain points that they’re gonna run into.” Adnan Alhaider — Owner/Manager, Footprints Floors Metro Detroit, Footprints Floors Insights More time back in the day to focus on customers, growth Today, more than 90% of Footprints Floors franchisees use CompanyCam to document, organize, and sell projects. Everyone, from Bryan and the corporate support staff to territory owners and install crews, works more efficiently and effectively, which leads to more jobs, more profitability, and sometimes more time back in their days to not be on job sites. As prospective franchisees undergo the validation process, current owners often bring up CompanyCam to underscore Bryan and Footprints’ commitment to their growth, protection, and development as owner-operators. 105 Users 587,521 Photos 32,418 Projects “[CompanyCam] helps the customer, it helps the franchisee, and, in the end, it ultimately grows us as a business.” Bryan Park — Founder & CEO, Footprints Floors See CompanyCam in Action Schedule a live walkthrough to see how it all works. Industry Windows & Doors Location Indianapolis Customer Since 2023 Favorite Feature Videos The Problem Insuring the business from costly claims Founded in 1930, McComb Window & Door is a fourth-generation custom window and door builder and installer — and the longest-tenured partner in the Pella Direct Sales Network (PDSN). They’ve grown to complete more than 2,500 projects annually in and around the Indianapolis area. The family of companies and branches in the Pella network regularly collaborate and share business ideas, so it was nothing new when Pella Windows & Doors of Omaha and Lincoln told McComb about CompanyCam. The product was presented to McComb as something that they (and all the PDSNs) could benefit from, especially when it comes to standardization, accountability, and liability protection. The window and door industry especially needs a product like CompanyCam because they, more than most trades, encounter situations where clients may try to blame them for things like leaks, cracked drywall, and damaged products. Having a solid documentation process meant not only visibility into their jobs but also protection from costly lawsuits. “You’re paying for protection in a way. So it’s kinda like you’re buying an insurance.” Dave Mathews — general manager, McComb Window and Door The Solution Utilizing more features for liability protection and growth Knowing that it was already a great fit for legal protection, Dave and his team found more ways to get the most out of their investment in CompanyCam. Their delivery crews take a photo of each line item product and its tag as they unload them at the job site so that they can ensure where and in what state the product is when it’s delivered or quickly identify if something is missing. Photo documentation has also become vital to managing working relationships with subcontractors. With CompanyCam as part of the process, subcontractors can see the photos captured by the sales and measurement teams and report any differences they see on site. “We’re trying to get to the point where we’re creating something where we’re not only taking job site photos… but also, from a legal standpoint, we’re protected because we’re showing what we’re doing on the job site.” Dave Mathews — general manager, McComb Window and Door Insights Planning for the future with confidence In the long term, McComb Window & Door plans to recreate its showroom experience within CompanyCam to help its sales representatives educate leads on the products and finishes they offer. With all their showrooms in the pocket of sales professionals, they hope to acquire new customers straight from their living rooms. They put their core values into action and began merging with other roofing and exterior operations, expanding to 17 offices in 10 states.Now, they have their sights set on becoming the country’s best customer service and experience in the roofing industry. “We want to know that we’re doing the same things on every job. One of the businesses they merged with used CompanyCam and advocated for it to be standardized across all Apple Roofing locations. CompanyCam joined Apple Roofing’s tech stack — along with their custom-built CRM and customer engagement platform — to help them run their day-to-day operations and facilitate communication between the office and the field. With custom-built, in-app checklists, they can ensure their team members are capturing everything that needs to be captured and relaying it to the office so that it can be reviewed and used for estimating bids and materials purchases. “When you’re talking about scaling, you have 200 people trying to do the same job, and you’re trying to set standards… the checklists that we’ve been able to create in CompanyCam are amazing.” Justin Martindale — sales manager, Apple Roofing Supporting Sales Their 130+ sales team members can better tell the story of Apple Roofing by showing customers photos of their own houses and, in great detail, what the homeowner needs to address. Photos instantly go from their in-app checklists to a field supervisor’s phone on the ground and the production coordinator’s computer screen in the office. This helps catch costly mistakes before they happen: A crew backs an empty trailer onto a customer’s lawn with permission. A supervisor sees that photo in CompanyCam and quickly calls the crew to tell them to move it because they know that once it’s loaded, they’ll, at best, leave four-inch ruts in the lawn and, at worst, be on the hook for a cracked driveway and broken sprinkler lines.
